name: Lia
animal-fact: The Vampire Moth is one of the few moth species that retains it's mouth after metamorphosis. Though a cute and fluffy creature that can subsist off fruit juice, this moth is known to have a painful bite and a taste for blood.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Lia, it’s been a while.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Nice of you to finally join us, daddy issues.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: What, no ‘hey, hello, how’s it been?’

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Is the lack of manners a sign of your mummy issues?

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Can’t have mummy issues if I don’t have a mother.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Unlike you.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: You’ve been collecting all the issues.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: You’re the worst. Is this why we never talked as kids?

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: We didn’t talk because I’m almost a decade older than you and you were a literal infant.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: You sound exactly like uncle Quan when you say that.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Sure do.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Who do you think you sound more like, your mum or your dad?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I don’t know? Why would you ask that?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Kind of insensitive.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: I’ve been told I have a tendency to pick at open wounds.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: In more than just the literal sense.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: You work with dead people, I know.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Forensic mortician is a lively job you know.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Aside from the obvious.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: It’s just a fun exploration of cause and effect.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: It makes me want to poke you with a cattle prod to see why you’re like this.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: That’s concerning?

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Most of us are pretty sure it’s your dad that's got you messed up, but I hypothesise that he’s just the symptom of your bigger problem.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: … I’m gonna regret this, but…

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: What is my bigger problem?

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Your crippling sense of inadequacy for one thing.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Your imposter syndrome – that’s somehow worse than Caspian’s – for another.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: But it mostly comes down to your crippling sense of self worth manifesting as consistent self-sabotage from all social relationships and then taking it as evidence that those relationships themselves were the problem and not you.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Argue with me. Come on, I’m ready for it.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: It was lovely talking to Lia, really.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I love catching up with you.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I’m gonna have to head off now, though… but, uh… catch you later.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: That’s a shame, I was looking forward to this.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: Ah well, can’t be helped.

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: I’ll see you around!

intro.speaker: Lia
intro.text: … probably soon.
